Flaw Text: The character is recovering from recent trauma, on edge, and the slightest knock can throw them into a bad place. Hitting rock bottom is far worse and/or far easier for this character, and in severe cases they may be a hair-trigger away from snapping.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Love, compassion, creativity, overwhelming emotion

Reversed: Blocked or repressed emotions

Flaw Text: Jilted ex-partner (business, family, romantic) has it in for the character, sabotaging or harassing them on a real-life level.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Unified love, partnership, attraction, relationships

Reversed: Break-up, imbalance in a relationship, lack of harmony

Flaw Text: The character is bad at keeping promises, notoriously so. Perhaps they're innately disloyal, the circumstances are weighted against them, or otherwise as befits the character. In any case, the character has a tendency or is forced to break promises, betray their group, sleep with the foe, so on so forth.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Celebration, friendship, creativity, community

Reversed: An affair, “three’s a crowd”, stifled creativity

Flaw Text: Veers wildly from one extreme to another, either because of emotional problems or an addiction. Mania/depression, obsessive love/hate, et cetera. Effort may be required to hide the affliction if one doesn't want their reputation to suffer with those close to them.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Meditation, contemplation, apathy, re-evaluation

Reversed: Boredom, missed opportunity, being aloof

Flaw Text: Character is trapped in the past, haunted by regret or loss, and they can't move on. Can be a code or pattern where the character acts in specific and less beneficial ways when dealing with specific people, or the character has a side mission that may conflict with the main one, related to making amends for something or helping someone else.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Loss, regret, disappointment, despair, bereavement

Reversed: Moving on, acceptance, forgiveness

Flaw Text: Shoots for the stars and misses. A single impossible goal, repeated attempts to reach higher than realistic, delusions of grandeur, or an addiction for that one-in-a-million shot. Success may spur them on their self-destructive path, while failure brings recklessness and other consequences to the point where only the similarly mad would want to stand by them.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Reunion, nostalgia, childhood memories, innocence

Reversed: Stuck in the past, naivety, unrealistic

Flaw Text: The character comes across as slippery, and not in a good way.  Appearance, speech patterns, rumours about their past, or they are the victim of an enemy's undermining. They don't get the benefit of a doubt and convincing others when it counts can be an uphill battle.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Fantasy, illusion, wishful thinking, choices, imagination

Reversed: Temptation, illusion, diversionary tactics

Flaw Text: The character is physically disabled. Either an arm or leg irrevocably twisted, a lung or heart condition inhibiting strenuous activity, or another physical disability of similar consequence as befits the character's background.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Escapism, disappointment, abandonment, withdrawal

Reversed: Hopelessness, aimless drifting, walking away

Flaw Text: The character is a materialist in the worst way. They're used to it or it's their means of filling a hole in their heart, the character is compelled to continually have *more*. They might be despondent if they can't upgrade their assets on the regular, fritter away income they shouldn't or money that isn't theirs, or be that much more inclined to betray their team for money. Money spent in this way is typically spent on luxuries or appearances.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Wishes fulfilled, comfort, happiness.

Reversed: Greed, dissatisfaction, materialism.

Flaw Text: The character's family is a problem. They're troublemakers and the character gets involved by association, they're rich and powerful and interfere in the character's doings, or they're doing battle on the same arenas the character is and they want very different things.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Harmony, marriage, happiness, alignment

Reversed: Misalignment, broken home.

Flaw Text: The character has a dependent whom they have to factor into every aspect of their life. A child too young to do anything for themself, a relative with severe mental issues, a disabled grandparent, something along those lines. This dependent is a sink for their money, time, and a weakness in case the character is targeted. Giving up on the dependent is not an option, either due to familial obligation, emotional ties, or otherwise. Losing this dependent inflicts severe penalties.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: A messenger, creative beginnings.

Reversed: Emotional immaturity, creative block.

Flaw Text: Character burns with resentment for someone else. They live in a shadow, and events conspire to keep them there while another individual thrives. Relegated to second in command, the lion's share of the rewards reaped by another. Removing the other guy is the only solution, and it isn't easy - the other person could be well protected, overpowering, or connected in a way that the character comes into conflict with themselves over removing the target.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Romance, charm, knight in shining armor, imagination.

Reversed: Unrealistic, jealousy, moodiness.

Flaw Text: The character doesn't 'get' normal people, whether by dint of their upbringing, social circles, physical and mental state, or power. Usually, they have a few points of friction with others due to differences in their day to day routine, which can result in arguments, fights, and allies being pushed away.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Emotional security, calm, intuitive, compassionate.

Reversed: Emotional insecurity, co-dependency.

Flaw Text:
The character believes their powers are more than a natural phenomenon and won't shut up about it. Magecraft, a deific touch, demons, or otherwise, their delusions cause people to be alienated, enforce certain compulsions on themselves, or otherwise.

As an alternative, the character was brought up around delusions and suffers from it. An antivaxxer parent, group of friends-turned-cultists, et cetera. This can have negative effects on their mental or physical health, or their past still haunts them to this day.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Emotional balance and control, generosity.

Reversed: Emotional manipulation, volatility.

Flaw Text: Something great was within their grasp, but the character fucked up. They're constantly reminded of their failure, whether this is a constant or something triggered by specific situations or images. They overthink their actions, potentially sabotaging themselves as they work toward their goals.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Manifestation, new financial opportunity

Reversed: Lost opportunity, lack of planning/foresight.

Flaw Text: Through some fault of the character or their life situation, the character has to juggle many - possibly conflicting - interests and ties. At first, this may give them simple objectives to work towards with minor penalties on failure, ramping up into character-defining decisions and lose-lose situations.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Balance, adaptability, time management.

Reversed: Disorganization, financial disarray.

Flaw Text: Whatever the character's past, they've dug themselves into a hole that they can't get out of. They may be part of a fracturing faction filled with infighting, a marriage gone sour and abusive, et cetera, but in all cases the character would rather go down with the ship than abandon it. When it finally collapses on the character, they suffer moderate penalties and move on to their next focus of obsession.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Gratitude, luxury, self-sufficiency.

Reversed: Over-investment in work, financial setbacks.

Flaw Text: Accumulated assets and money are spent fast, perhaps faster than they can be gained. The character might be a partier, not a savvy saver, or have other issues that result in this flaw. Their hard assets have a tendency to be lost or broken, they impulse buy or have to waste a lot of money on some habit, they misplace important information, et cetera.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Manifestation, financial opportunity, new role

Reversed: Lack of progress, short-term focus.

Flaw Text: Either on a macro, or a micro level, you're inflexible. Could be your worldview, sticking to how you were raised, whether it be staunch traditionalist values, or someone who believes the past should be discarded completely. You might have certain touchstones - a weapon, a piece of clothing, a diner - which if threatened or destroyed, inflicts penalities on you.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Efficiency, routine, conservatism.

Reversed: Laziness, boredom, feeling 'stuck'.

Flaw Text: Think Starscream. The character has an intrinsic need to be at the top. They feel a deep urge to take down the guy at the top and instate themselves in that place. This often starts at insults or insubordination, but they must continually top prior acts of disrespect - oft leading to outright backstabbing. If already at the top of their group, the itch will return as they aim towards expanding their group's control, even to the extent of city-wide dominance.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Security, power, control, discipline, abundance.

Reversed: Authoritative, controlling, domineering.

Flaw Text: When the character tries to do something, there is always an opposing force that halts their efforts. This can be an adversary actively working to undercut them, a social group engineering things against their favour, or something else entirely as befits the character. The character may have to spend more resources, time, effort and/or manpower to deal with this resistive force.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Indecision, choices, truce, stalemates, blocked emotion.

Reversed: Indecision, confusion, information overload.

Flaw Text: Some people are their own worst enemies, and this character is that in a nutshell. Self-destructive behaviour defines a majority of their attitude and actions. While working towards their goals, they may find themselves taking actions that run counter to said goals.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Contemplation, recuperation, passivity, relaxation.

Reversed: Restlessness, burn-out, lack of progress.

Flaw Text: Something horrible is buried in the character's subconscious and they are not aware of it. They may have repressed this trauma, been forced to forget, or aren't aware of the true importance of something major. In any case, this will come back to haunt them in the future, for example from others in the know coming after them.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Regretful but necessary transition, rite of passage.

Reversed: Cannot move on, carrying baggage

Flaw Text: A sword of Damocles hangs over the character's head, or they think it does. There's a likelihood that the character will be betrayed by allies, and the betrayal will be bad. They may alternatively think that such a betrayal is coming and push others away, distancing themselves from allies in a time of need.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Betrayal, deception, getting away with something, stealth.

Reversed: Mental challenges, breaking free.

Flaw Text: The character is bound by a code or ideology they have a hard time straying from. Inability to hurt a group, religious extremism, unwillingness to attack those of a certain caliber, et cetera. Failing to stick to this code or ideology results in penalties, severity determined by how badly the code was broken.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Isolation, self-imposed restriction.

Reversed: Open to new perspectives, release.

Flaw Text: Roll on the power flaw chart twice. Such was the impact of the character’s personality, lifestyle, history, or otherwise on the way their power manifested.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Depression, nightmares, anxiety.

Reversed: Hopelessness, depression, torment.

Flaw Text: For some reason innate or learnt, the character is hard to get along with. Their voice and mannerisms may set people on edge, their attitude or beliefs may be extreme, or something else fitting the character. In any case, this makes working with the character *hard* for extended periods of time.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Talkative, curious, mentally restless.

Reversed: Bark without bite, haste making waste, undelivered promises.

Flaw Text: The character begins the game addicted to drugs or other such addictive substances. Alcohol, nicotine, cocaine, meth, heroin are common choices. This is socially detrimental, a draw on their income, or damages their mind and body. Having access to a steady supply of drugs may ameliorate these issues on a surface level while digging the character a deeper hole overall.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Opinionated, hasty, action-oriented, communicative.

Reversed: Scattered thought, disregard for consequences.

Flaw Text: There are some who are calming to be around - this character is the opposite. Whether because they're abrasive or unpleasant, incapable of normal socialization or something in that vein, others would require the patience of a saint to put up with them. They may even exaggerate the issues of others by being around them.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Quick thinking, organized, perceptive, independent.

Reversed: Overly emotional, bitchy, cold-hearted.

Flaw Text: The character is in a precarious position, posed to collapse dramatically at some point. A toxic, volatile group may form around them, they acquire many clashing responsibilities, they take on too many tasks, et cetera. When this comes to a head, the character is left devastated and recovery is slow, if possible.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Clear thought, intellectual power, authority, truth.

Reversed: Manipulative, tyrannical, abusive.

Flaw Text: The character is interested in self-pleasure above all else. This tends to impact their priorities and their physical condition. May include regular imbibing of drugs, reckless and ill-advised sexual liasons, or overindulgence in other things, such as food. They typically have multiple vices rather than one major one, favoring a breadth of riskier experiences over an obsessive attachment to one, their body likely bears the scars or effects of past indulgences, and/or they regularly prioritize pleasure over responsibility, with the fallout that follows.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Inspiration, power, creation

Reversed: Delays, lack of motivation, burdens.

Flaw Text: The character lacks the ability to read, write, or use technology. This can be from brain injuries, a power factor, or just sheer inability. Alternatively, they are socially illiterate, unable to notice the 'mood' of a group and read the context. Depending on character, can manifest in multiple aspects.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Celebration, home, community.

Reversed: Breakdown in communication, transitions.

Flaw Text: The character is a coward, though they may call themselves "risk-averse" or "conflict-avoidant". Taking early hits or being near to the center of a fight spooks the character, or they look towards running from a fight more often than not, or otherwise as fits the backstory.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Disagreement, competition, strife, tension and conflict.

Reversed: Conflict avoidance, diversity, unhappy compromise.

Flaw Text: The character went from bad to worse, and the blame lies with them and their choices. The player picks a life flaw and gains it, doubled down.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Innocence, spontaneity, free spirit, potential.

Reversed: Naivety, foolishness, risk-taking.

Flaw Text: The character has a severe mental illness of some sort. Schizophrenia, obsessive compulsive disorder, a cognitive impairment, or otherwise. In any case, it either lowers their general quality of life, makes one type of task nigh impossible, or both.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Power, skill, concentration, action

Reversed: Manipulation, poor planning, latent talents

Flaw Text: The character's ability to speak or communicate is hampered.  May be unable to talk altogether, but expressive with body language or writing, might need assistance via. devices, have a language barrier, or simply struggle on a consistent basis due to speech/writing difficulties across the board.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Fertility, femininity, beauty, nature

Reversed: Creative block, dependence on others

Flaw Text: The character is subservient to another, and this other demands something from them consistently. A family member, a gang with blackmail over the character, a cluster member who holds some critical weakness in their hands. Getting out of the clutches of this demanding other is hard, even impossible, and failing to provide offerings will result in punishments, sabotaged plans, et cetera.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Authority, father-figure, structure, foundation.

Reversed: Domination, excessive control, rigidity, inflexibility

Flaw Text: The character is damaged, hollowed out, depressed and vulnerable due to past trauma. Detrimental emotional states stay with them longer, they lash out at friends and family, they are particularly vulnerable to mental and emotional power effects, or otherwise as fits the character.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Strength, courage, patience, control, compassion

Reversed: Weakness, self-doubt, lack of discipline

Flaw Text: Change comes sudden, and not all can handle it, certainly not this character. Some great upheaval in their past cut the narrative of their life into tatters, severing connections and support systems they would have had. As a result, the character starts off isolated, becomes withdrawn, has recurring trauma, or something fitting the devastating change they underwent.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Soul searching, introspection, being alone, inner guidance

Reversed: Isolation, loneliness, withdrawal

Flaw Text: An issue consumes the individual, but it's not all bad. Roll again for life flaw. Double down on the strength of the resulting flaw. Roll three times for a life perk. GM vetoes one and player vetoes one. Take remaining pick.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Good luck, karma, life cycles, destiny, a turning point

Reversed: Bad luck, negative external forces, out of control

Flaw Text: This character did something bad, or someone thinks they did. From the outset, they are stuck with the negative reputation of someone repulsive, dangerous, or more. Steps can be taken to alleviate this reputation, but their past will catch up in one way or another.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Justice, fairness, truth, cause-and-effect, law

Reversed: Unfairness, lack of accountability, dishonesty

Flaw Text: Something is out for the character, be it an influential public figure, a specific group, their history, or the effects of their power. This leaves the character outclassed, outgunned, out of their depth, with large incentives for those that manage to take them down. Surviving with this mark on their head is taxing and escape is most likely impossible.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Endings, beginnings, change, transformation, transition

Reversed: Resistance to change, unable to move on.

Flaw Text: The character is a train wreck, and they have a way of making their problems into other people's problems. Drama surrounds them or they ruin people they interact with. Roll another life flaw. That flaw is manageable by diverting the negative energies at others, making people around them miserable.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Balance, moderation, patience, purpose

Reversed: Imbalance, excess, lack of long-term vision

Flaw Text: The character has an opponent in the area with a vendetta, either working actively against the character or manipulating things behind the scenes. Failing to deal with the nemesis will result in continual encounters with them, with consequences such as damaged assets, severe wounds, allies found mutilated, or even death.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Bondage, addiction, sexualism, materialism

Reversed: Detachment, breaking free, power reclaimed

Flaw Text: The character has a secret that threatens to ruin them.  Should it come out, ripple effects will affect them in reputation, power, available assets, or psychological well being. A weak point, either literal or psychological, a trauma of their past, or a dark deed they once committed, of the sort that would turn even scummy villain friends into serious enemies. Others who get close to the character may gradually pick up on clues, including enemies who associate with them with any frequency.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Disaster, upheaval, sudden change, revelation

Reversed: Avoiding disaster, fear of change

Flaw Text: The character suffers from a deep internal conflict, and the two ideals or beliefs in contest are not easily given up or reconciled. Depending on what the nature of this internal conflict is, the character develops negative quirks or a tendency to take self-destructive actions as befits the conflict, or otherwise.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Hope, spirituality, renewal, serenity

Reversed: Lack of faith, despair, discouragement

Flaw Text: The character has a number of mental hang-ups and assorted traumas that hamper their ability to act. Depending on the nature of their mental obstacles, the character may be unable to discuss or listen to certain topics, develop odd tendencies as befits the trauma, or otherwise.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Illusion, fear, anxiety, insecurity, subconscious

Reversed: Release of fear, unhappiness, confusion

Flaw Text: The character is hampered by a physical problem, real or imagined. Depending on the nature of this flaw, the character does worse in specific physical challenges, develops negative lines of thought, or otherwise as fits the problem at hand.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Fun, warmth, success, positivity, vitality

Reversed: Temporary depression, lack of success

Flaw Text: Some part of the character's self-identity is hatred and loathing, and it is all directed at a specific idea or abstract concept. This can be anything from a political philosophy to an approach to life that they just can't stand. When facing such, the character reacts with anger, violence, or some other fitting negativity.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Judgement, rebirth, inner calling, absolution

Reversed: Self doubt, refusal to look inward

Flaw Text: Something is fundamentally wrong about this character. A part of their basic morality breaks core human precepts, common sensibility and decency, the unwritten rules, or some combination of the above and more. This violation may be so bad that even the worst villain factions find the character repulsive to be around, a tool to be used at best.
CARD MEANING:
Upright: Completion, accomplishment

Reversed: Incomplete, lack of closure
